title: podman load image fails due to small /var
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am trying to load a docker image previously in use on our server before being upgraded from RH7 to RH8. The load command fails due to podman trying to create the image in /var/tmp, which on our server is very small. I have tried to specify a larger volume to do this in using the TMPDIR variable, but it seems when I run the command it is trying to use /var/tmp still. How can I get podman to load into a larger space?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set up server with teeny tiny /var

2. Set TMPDIR to /appdata (bigger place)

3. podman load -i my_image.tar.gz

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: initializing source oci:megaqc_all4.tar.gz:: open megaqc_all4.tar.gz/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: creating temp directory: untarring file "/var/tmp/oci1076929110": write /var/tmp/oci1076929110/acc7fe3f25d412d95095bec1e303cf70e5a25aec1c29ec7b83d324d7be91ce41/layer.tar: no space left on device
 * docker-archive: copying contents to temporary file "/var/tmp/docker-tar2345326614": write /var/tmp/docker-tar2345326614: no space left on device
 * dir: open megaqc_all4.tar.gz/manifest.json: not a directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected the docker image to load as previous

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Tue Apr 19 11:16:32 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
/amd64
```

comments.body: @rhatdan PTAL - you've dealt with TMPDIR before.

I realised this is a sudo/user problem, not a podman issue, so will close this issue. The other part of the error message is still present though (the image being supposedly invalid) so I will make a new issue for that.
